3) For a bipartite graph, the minimum vertex cover equals the size of the maximum matching. In this case, since the graph is cubic and bipartite with 54 nodes, the maximum matching would include 27 edges.

4) The minimum vertex cover would include one endpoint from each of these 27 matched edges. Since we have a perfect matching in this cubic bipartite graph, we can choose either all nodes from Set A or all nodes from Set B as our minimum vertex cover.
